task.title: Run a Collection
task.shortdesc: Use the Collection Runner to execute all requests in a collection in sequence and review the aggregated test results.

prereq.p: Before you run a collection, ensure that:

ul.li: The collection contains at least one request with test scripts.
An environment is configured with the correct variable values for the target system.

context.p: The Collection Runner executes all requests in a collection in the order they appear in the sidebar. After the run completes, Postman displays a summary of all test results, including the number of tests that passed and failed.

step.cmd: In the sidebar, hover over the collection name and select the Run collection icon (the arrow icon), or right-click the collection and select Run collection.
In the Environment dropdown, select the environment to use for the run.
In the Iterations field, enter the number of times to repeat the run. Leave this set to 1 for a single run.
In the Delay field, enter a delay in milliseconds between requests if the API requires rate limiting. A value of 0 sends requests with no delay.
To run only specific requests, clear the checkboxes next to the requests you want to skip in the request list.
Select Run (the button labeled with your collection name).
step.stepresult: Postman sends each request in sequence. The runner panel updates in real time, showing each request as it completes with a pass or fail indicator.

step.cmd: After the run completes, review the results summary at the top of the runner panel.
To view the details of a specific request, select the request name in the results list.
step.stepresult: Postman displays the request details, response, and individual test assertion results for the selected request.

step.cmd: To export the results as a JSON file, select Export Results.

r.p: The Collection Runner executes all requests and displays a pass or fail result for each test assertion. A green indicator next to the collection name in the summary indicates all tests passed. A red indicator means one or more tests failed.
